events.title: Bookbinding Workshop with Pashariko Bookbinding
events.description:
Bookbinding workshop with with Pashariko Bookbinding using all secondhand papers and materials.
Learn some basic bookbinding with all secondhand papers and materials! In this evening workshop, we’ll be focusing in on the pamphlet. The pamphlet can become a little notebook for your pocket, a fun way to level up a zine, a perfect format for an artist book, a sweet gift, and so much more! We’ll talk about paper, different variations on the pamphlet stitch, and fun ways you can personalize and decorate your books. Spending an evening with your book has never been more hands on 🙂
This workshop is hosted at Brooklyn Creative Reuse. 254 36th Street, 2nd Floor, Space C244. May 27th, 7-10pm.
events.vendor: Bookbinding Workshop with Pashariko Bookbinding

events.title: The Comics Blueprint
events.description:
Refine your ideas or explore a new story surrounded by peers in this 2 week comics workshop led by writer and illustrator, Davis Cathcart.
In this comics workshop, students will learn how to put their story goals before their visuals in order to understand the fundamentals of visual storytelling and how the story drives imagery when the two are working in tandem. Students will execute on the ins and outs of story structure, character and telling a narrative in the cuts.
Instructor Bio: Davis Cathcart is an illustrator and writer currently residing in Ridgewood, Queens, New York. He has studied art and illustration at the Cooper Union, Maryland Institute College of Art, Rhode Island School of Design (RISD), and the School of Visual Arts. He has worked for Maira Kalman, William Wegman, and The New Yorker’s Art Department. Davis has taught art to children of all ages in public schools all over New York City. Both RISD and the Society of Illustrators have given him awards for his illustration work. He likes telling cerebral stories that do not take themselves too seriously and tries to subvert the carefree aspects of his writing with the detail of his visuals. He plans to continue to publish his work and teach.
events.vendor: The Comics Blueprint

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting

events.title: Real Young & Lazy Horse Ride to Industry City!
events.description:
Tickets Here
Each ticket purchase includes 12-ounce beer, 7 ounce wine, well cocktail or Non-alcoholic beverage. Additional beverages can be purchased for reasonable prices!
Industry City and Get It Right Music in association with Murmrr Presents are proud to welcome Real Young & Lazy Horse for a late Spring show at our outdoor bandshell right in the band’s home neighborhood of Sunset Park, Brooklyn!
We are beyond thrilled to host this band! In their own words, “Real Young & Lazy Horse are an unreasonably faithful, occasionally feral reincarnation of Neil Young & Crazy Horse at their most ragged and transcendent. Drawing from the deep well of Neil’s catalog, the band doesn’t play the songs so much as become them.
“Expect epic, long-burn renditions stacked with solos that ride past the horizon line — just as the spirit of the Horse intends. With shows routinely spilling over the three-hour mark it is nothing short of a phenomenon to experience this uncanny sensation. Real Young & Lazy Horse. Don’t be denied!”
Event Info
Date: May 30, 2026
Doors: 2:00pm
Set 1: 3:00 pm
Set 2: 5:00pm
Tickets: $20 per person plus fees in advance/$25 plus fees day of show (all tickets include one 12-ounce beer, 7 ounce wine, well cocktail or Non-alcoholic beverage.)
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
events.vendor: Real Young & Lazy Horse Ride to Industry City!

events.title: Introduction to Sake and Brewery Tour
events.description:
If you’ve wanted to learn the basics of sake in a fun and approachable way, this Introduction to Sake tasting class is for you! Join us in person at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura in NYC to learn the basics of sake and enjoy a guided tasting of the most delicious sakes. We invite you to taste and learn about sake where sake is made! Get a guided brewery tour, taste and explore a set of 5 premium sakes including some fresh from the press! Let’s level up your sake knowledge and have fun doing it!
What’s included:
A welcome sake to get you started!
Sake 101 presentation to cover all the basics
Guided Tasting of 4 Brooklyn Kura craft sakes as well as a premium sake from Japan.
Handouts so you can bring your sake knowledge home
Tasting some sake fresh from the press!
Guided tour through our new Sake Brewery
Some nibbles to pair with your sakes
events.vendor: Introduction to Sake and Brewery Tour

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

Slide through for more information, including details of a special vinyl variant ONLY available at these Release Parties.
*Free while supplies last

***No purchase necessary, must be present to win.
THE BOYS OF DUNGEON LANE from Paul McCartney, is released May 29 on CD and Vinyl.

events.title: Professional Sake Training: Sake Server Certification
events.description:
Event Details
Sign up for this class At the Sake Studies Center:
https://www.sakestudiescenter.com/courses#id=sake-server-certification
This is a one day in person seminar from 10am to 5pm with a 1 hour lunch break.
Level up your service skills in sake! This one day sake training certification course will teach you all the ins and outs of proper service and sales for sake in your your restaurant or retail shop. There is no prerequisite and this course is open to anyone wishing to get training in professional service of sake. The course consists of four modules.
1) Introduction to sake & Brewery Tour
2) sake sales techniques
3) sake service and presentation
4) sake menus and pairing
After the one day training course in completed, you’ll have to option to complete an online course review and online exam within two weeks time. Upon successful completion of the review and exam, you’ll receive the Sake Studies Center “Sake Server Certification” diploma.
As a graduate of our professional Sake Server Certification, you’ll also have ongoing access to our free online sake community for the sake industry. Ask questions, and stay in touch with your fellow sake students and industry leaders.
Meet your Teacher
Timothy Sullivan is the Director of Education and lead instructor for classes at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura. He has over 16 years of experience teaching about sake and in 2007, he was awarded the prestigious title of Sake Samurai by the Japan Sake Brewers Association. Timothy brings a wealth of knowledge as well as practical brewing experience to the classroom. Whether for sake beginners or for industry professionals, Timothy strives to make all his classes and tasting fun and engaging.
events.vendor: Professional Sake Training: Sake Server Certification
